name: MailerLite
description: MailerLite email marketing — manage subscribers, groups, segments, campaigns, automations, forms, custom fields, and webhooks. Connect your own MailerLite API key to read and manage your own account's data here. Some features are plan-gated by MailerLite itself (e.g. resend/multivariate campaigns, advanced automation triggers) — this extension detects and reports that honestly rather than guessing.

tools.name: skeleton_refresh_mailerlite_config
tools.description: MailerLite connection status — whether the user has connected an API key

tools.name: skeleton_refresh_mailerlite_groups
tools.description: The user's MailerLite groups — name and subscriber counts (up to 5 shown)

tools.name: connection_status
tools.description: Whether the user's own MailerLite API key is connected. Use for: is my MailerLite account connected, connection status.

tools.name: save_mailerlite_key
tools.description: Connect a MailerLite account by saving its API key. Validates the key against MailerLite before saving. Use for: connect my MailerLite account, save this API key.

mailerlite_api_key.description: Your MailerLite API key, from MailerLite -> Integrations -> MailerLite API -> Generate new token
mailerlite_api_key.title: Mailerlite Api Key

label.description: Optional display name for this account
label.title: Label

tools.name: list_mailerlite_accounts
tools.description: List every MailerLite account you've connected — label, masked key, which one is active.

tools.name: switch_mailerlite_account
tools.description: Switch which connected MailerLite account is active — all following calls use this one.

tools.name: disconnect_mailerlite_account
tools.description: Disconnect ONE specific connected MailerLite account by its label — removes only that key.

tools.name: list_subscribers
tools.description: List subscribers, optionally filtered by status (active/unsubscribed/unconfirmed/bounced/junk). Use for: list my subscribers, show active subscribers.

status.description: Filter: active, unsubscribed, unconfirmed, bounced, junk
status.title: Status

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous page's response
cursor.title: Cursor

tools.name: create_fill_subscriber
tools.description: Create or update (upsert) a subscriber by email. Non-destructive — omitted fields/groups are NOT removed from an existing subscriber. Use for: add this email to my list.

fields.description: Custom field values, e.g. {'name': 'Jane'}. Never removes omitted fields.
fields.title: Fields

groups.description: Group ids to add this subscriber to. Never removes omitted groups.
groups.title: Groups

status.description: active, unsubscribed, unconfirmed, bounced, or junk
status.title: Status

tools.name: get_subscriber
tools.description: Fetch one subscriber by id. Use for: get subscriber details.

tools.name: subscriber_activity
tools.description: A subscriber's engagement activity (opens, clicks, etc). Use for: subscriber engagement history.

tools.name: add_subscriber_to_group
tools.description: Assign an existing subscriber to a group.

tools.name: remove_subscriber_from_group
tools.description: Remove a subscriber from a group.

tools.name: delete_subscriber
tools.description: Permanently delete a subscriber.

tools.name: forget_subscriber
tools.description: GDPR 'right to be forgotten': permanently erases this subscriber's personal data (distinct from delete_subscriber, which just removes the record — forget is MailerLite's own compliance endpoint, POST /subscribers/{id}/forget, confirmed via MailerLite's official SDK source). Use for: forget this subscriber, right to be forgotten.

tools.name: list_groups
tools.description: List mailing groups. Use for: list my groups.

tools.name: create_group
tools.description: Create a new mailing group. Use for: new group called X.

tools.name: rename_group
tools.description: Rename an existing mailing group.

tools.name: delete_group
tools.description: Permanently delete a group (subscribers stay, just un-grouped).

tools.name: group_subscribers
tools.description: List subscribers belonging to a group.

tools.name: list_segments
tools.description: List dynamic subscriber segments. NOTE: segment filter rules are built in the MailerLite UI only — this extension can list/rename/delete existing segments but not author new filter logic. Use for: list my segments.

tools.name: rename_segment
tools.description: Rename a segment (filter rules themselves can't be edited via API).

tools.name: delete_segment
tools.description: Permanently delete a segment.

tools.name: segment_subscribers
tools.description: List subscribers matching a segment's rules.

tools.name: list_campaigns
tools.description: List campaigns filtered by status (sent/draft/ready) and/or type (regular/ab/resend/rss). Use for: list my campaigns, show draft/sent campaigns.

status.description: sent, draft, or ready (defaults to ready)
status.title: Status

limit.description: Must be one of 1, 10, 25, 50, or 100 (confirmed live) — any other value is rejected
limit.title: Limit

tools.name: get_campaign
tools.description: Fetch one campaign by id. Use for: get this campaign.

tools.name: create_campaign
tools.description: Create a draft campaign. type='resend'/'multivariate' and content_html both carry a REAL risk of being rejected on lower MailerLite plans — any such rejection is surfaced with MailerLite's own exact wording, never guessed. Use for: draft a new campaign called X.

type.description: regular, ab, resend, or multivariate. 'resend' and 'multivariate' are documented by MailerLite as available only on growing/advanced-tier plans — expect a possible plan-restricted rejection on lower plans, surfaced verbatim from MailerLite's own error.
type.title: Type

from_email.description: Must be an email address already verified in MailerLite
from_email.title: From Email

reply_to.description: Must be an email address already verified in MailerLite
reply_to.title: Reply To

content_html.description: Raw HTML email body. Observed live: MailerLite can reject this with "Content submission is only available on Premium plan." on non-Premium accounts — surfaced verbatim, never silently swallowed.
content_html.title: Content Html

tools.name: schedule_campaign
tools.description: Schedule a ready campaign to send (or send it instantly with delivery='instant'). Use for: schedule this campaign for tomorrow, send now.

schedule_type.description: unix_timestamp or timezone-relative — see MailerLite docs
schedule_type.title: Schedule Type

tools.name: cancel_campaign
tools.description: Cancel a campaign that's currently in 'ready' state (queued to send). Use for: cancel this send.

tools.name: delete_campaign
tools.description: Permanently delete a campaign. Use for: delete this campaign.

tools.name: list_automations
tools.description: List automations (name, enabled/disabled, created date) — read-only. MailerLite's API can't build or edit automation steps; those are UI-only. Use for: list my automations, show automation workflows.

tools.name: get_automation
tools.description: Fetch one automation's basic info by id. Use for: get this automation.

tools.name: automation_activity
tools.description: Subscriber activity flowing through an automation, filtered by status (completed/active/canceled/failed — required). Use for: who's in this automation, automation subscriber activity.

status.description: completed, active, canceled, or failed (required by MailerLite)
status.title: Status

tools.name: create_automation_draft
tools.description: Create an EMPTY draft automation (name only) — MailerLite's API can't define triggers/steps, so the user still needs to open MailerLite's UI to actually build the workflow. Use for: create a draft automation shell.

name.description: Draft name only — MailerLite's API cannot define an automation's trigger/steps; those must still be built in the MailerLite UI after this draft is created.
name.title: Name

tools.name: delete_automation
tools.description: Permanently delete an automation. Use for: delete this automation.

tools.name: list_forms
tools.description: List forms of one type: popup, embedded, or promotion (required — MailerLite has no combined 'all forms' endpoint). Use for: list my popup/embedded forms.

form_type.description: popup, embedded, or promotion (required — path segment, not a filter)
form_type.title: Form Type

tools.name: rename_form
tools.description: Rename a form. Use for: rename this form.

tools.name: delete_form
tools.description: Permanently delete a form. Use for: delete this form.

tools.name: form_subscribers
tools.description: List subscribers who signed up via a specific form. Use for: subscribers from this form.

tools.name: list_fields
tools.description: List custom subscriber fields (text/number/date). Use for: list custom fields.

tools.name: create_field
tools.description: Create a custom subscriber field (type: text, number, or date). Use for: add a custom field.

tools.name: rename_field
tools.description: Rename a custom field. Use for: rename this field.

tools.name: delete_field
tools.description: Permanently delete a custom field. Use for: delete this field.

tools.name: list_webhooks
tools.description: List configured webhooks. Use for: list my webhooks.

tools.name: create_webhook
tools.description: Create a webhook for a MailerLite event (e.g. subscriber.created, campaign.sent — see MailerLite docs for the full event list). campaign.click/campaign.open/subscriber.deleted REQUIRE batchable=true (MailerLite's own requirement). Use for: add a webhook for this event.

events.description: One or more MailerLite webhook event names, e.g. subscriber.created, campaign.sent. campaign.click, campaign.open, and subscriber.deleted REQUIRE batchable=true (MailerLite's own rule).
events.title: Events

url.description: Your endpoint URL that will receive the webhook POST
url.title: Url

tools.name: delete_webhook
tools.description: Permanently delete a webhook. Use for: delete this webhook.

tools.name: list_campaign_languages
tools.description: List valid language_id values for campaign unsubscribe-page language.

tools.name: list_timezones
tools.description: List valid timezone_id values for scheduling campaigns.

tools.name: mailerlite_capability_status
tools.description: Show this MailerLite account's REAL plan (name, price, subscriber tier, smart-sending flag — read directly from MailerLite's own account endpoint) plus any capability restrictions actually observed from live API responses, and MailerLite's own documented plan gates for things the account endpoint doesn't itself describe (e.g. whether HTML campaign content is unlocked at this tier). Use for: what MailerLite features am I limited to, plan limits.

PlanInfo.description:
The account's REAL plan, read directly from GET /account — confirmed
live to return real plan data (200 OK) even though it isn't in the
documented endpoint list at developers.mailerlite.com. This is ground
truth when the call succeeds; GATED_HINTS/cached verdicts remain the
fallback for sub-capabilities /account doesn't itself describe (e.g.
whether HTML content submission is unlocked at this exact tier).
PlanInfo.title: PlanInfo

secrets.name: mailerlite_accounts
secrets.description: Every MailerLite account you've connected (JSON list of {label, api_key, is_active}) — lets you track multiple MailerLite accounts and switch between them. Managed only through this extension's own connect/switch/disconnect actions.

file_sinks.description: Turn an uploaded HTML email template into a new draft campaign
